Q20 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
1. Flexibility matrix is difficult to form for large and complex structure.
2. Flexibility matrix is applicable for stable structures only.
3. Stiffness method is preferred over flexibility method for computer analysis.
4. Stiffness matrix method holds good for determinate structures only.
Which of the above statements are correct ?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Stiffness matrix method is applicable for both determinate and indeterminate structures.

Q23 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Statement (I): Muller - Breslau principle is an axiom that can be established using the principle of virtual work.
Statement (II): A body subjected to an imaginary virtual motion will experience zero work due to all forces and moments acting on the body.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
An axiom is something that is self-proved and does not require any specific proof. However,
Muller – Breslau principle is required to be proved using principle of virtual work for its correctness.
Hence Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.
Statement (I) would have been true had the word axiom not been there.

Q29 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

In a plane truss, counter brace is the member:

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
A counter brace in a plane truss is a member designed to resist both tensile and compressive
forces, depending on the load conditions and the direction of the applied forces. In a truss, some
members are designed primarily for tension (ties) and others for compression (struts). However,
a counter brace is specifically included to handle stress reversals that can occur due to varying
load conditions (e.g., live loads, wind loads).

Which of the above statements are correct ?

The following items consists of two statements, one labelled as Statement (I) and the other labelled as Statement (II). You have to examine these two statements carefully and select your answers to these items using the codes given below:
Codes:
(a) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are true and Statement (II) is the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(b) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are true but Statement (II) is not a correct explanation of Statement (I).
(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

Q36 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

The following items consists of two statements, one labelled as Statement (I) and the other labelled as Statement (II). You have to examine these two statements carefully and select your answers to these items using the codes given below:
Codes:
(a) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are true and Statement (II) is the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(b) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are true but Statement (II) is not a correct explanation of Statement (I).
(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

Statement (I): When a member is fixed at one end and simply supported at other end then an applied moment at simply supported end will indus one-third moment at the fixed end of the member.
Statement (II): The induced moment at the fixed end will be in the same direction as that of applied moment at the simply supported end.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Moment induced at fixed end will be one half of the moment applied at simply supported end and thus, Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.
